detection_pattern: Monitor for: kernel panic or OOPS messages referencing vow driver; unusual syscall sequences with rapid ioctl calls to vow device; memory sanitizer warnings (KASAN/MSAN) showing use-after-free in vow driver code; multiple processes simultaneously accessing /dev/vow*; abnormal kernel memory allocation patterns preceding privilege escalation; SELinux/AppArmor audit logs showing unexpected vow driver capability usage.
mitigation_strategy: Apply kernel patch for vow driver race condition fix; add proper synchronization primitives (mutex/spinlock) around memory allocation and access; implement reference counting for driver resources; enable KASAN/SMACK at runtime for detection; restrict vow device access via DAC permissions (0600) and SELinux policy; disable vow driver if unused; keep kernel and drivers fully patched; monitor syscall tracing of vow ioctl operations for anomalies.

threat_vector: HTML injection via MAIN_MAX_DECIMALS_TOT parameter in admin/limits.php (Dolibarr 7.0.2)
exploitation_method: Attacker submits malicious HTML/JavaScript payload through the MAIN_MAX_DECIMALS_TOT configuration parameter. The application fails to sanitize or escape user input before rendering it in the admin interface, allowing script execution in admin context.
detection_pattern: Monitor for: (1) POST requests to admin/limits.php with HTML/script tags in parameter values; (2) Log entries showing MAIN_MAX_DECIMALS_TOT containing <, >, or javascript: patterns; (3) Admin interface responses containing unescaped user input; (4) Session hijacking attempts following admin page loads; (5) Unusual database configuration changes tied to admin/limits.php requests
mitigation_strategy: Apply output encoding to all configuration parameters displayed in admin/limits.php using htmlspecialchars() or equivalent context-appropriate escaping. Implement strict input validation on MAIN_MAX_DECIMALS_TOT to accept only numeric values. Deploy Content Security Policy (CSP) headers to prevent inline script execution. Update to patched Dolibarr version (7.0.3+). Restrict admin/limits.php access via IP whitelisting and enforce strong admin authentication.

threat_vector: Improper sanitization of incoming intent in Dressroom prior to SMR Jan-2022 Release 1 allows local attackers to read and write arbitrary files without permission
exploitation_method: Attacker crafts malicious intent with unsanitized path traversal sequences (../, etc.) or special characters. The vulnerable Dressroom component fails to validate or escape intent parameters before passing them to file operations, allowing arbitrary file reads and writes on the system with the application's privileges.
detection_pattern: Monitor for: (1) Intent objects containing path traversal sequences like '../' or absolute paths in Dressroom component logs; (2) File access violations or permission denials for unexpected file paths; (3) Unexpected file modifications outside application data directories; (4) SELinux/AppArmor denials related to Dressroom; (5) Spike in file operation syscalls with suspicious paths; (6) Local process attempting file operations on system directories or /data/data paths of other applications
mitigation_strategy: Upgrade Dressroom to SMR Jan-2022 Release 1 or later. Implement strict input validation on all intent parameters using whitelist-based path canonicalization. Use realpath() to resolve paths and verify they remain within intended directories. Apply principle of least privilege to application file permissions. Implement SELinux policy to restrict file access scope. Validate and sanitize all path components before file I/O operations.

threat_vector: MediaWiki WikibaseMediaInfo XSS via caption fields (CVE-2021-41801)
exploitation_method: Attacker injects malicious JavaScript code into caption fields of media files in WikibaseMediaInfo. When other users view the media file page, the unescaped caption content executes in their browser context, allowing session hijacking, credential theft, or wiki modification.
detection_pattern: Monitor for: (1) HTTP POST/PUT requests to WikibaseMediaInfo caption endpoints containing script tags, event handlers (onclick, onerror, onload), or JavaScript protocol handlers; (2) Unusual characters in caption data (angle brackets, quotes, backslashes in unexpected sequences); (3) WAF logs showing XSS payloads in mediainfo API calls; (4) Browser console errors indicating injected scripts; (5) Audit logs showing caption field modifications with suspicious content patterns
mitigation_strategy: Upgrade MediaWiki to version 1.35.5, 1.36.3, 1.37.1 or later immediately. Implement output encoding/escaping for all caption field rendering in WikibaseMediaInfo templates. Apply Content Security Policy (CSP) headers restricting script-src to trusted sources. Validate and sanitize all user input to caption fields server-side using a whitelist approach. Implement a WAF rule to block requests containing common XSS patterns in media caption parameters. Review existing media file captions in database for injected payloads and remediate.

threat_vector: Improper input validation in Shockwall system programming function allows authenticated LAN attacker to send malicious responses disrupting server operations
exploitation_method: Attacker with LAN access crafts malformed packets or responses targeting the programming function endpoint. Lacks input sanitization allows injection of unexpected data types, oversized payloads, or special characters that trigger buffer overflows, command injection, or state corruption in the server process.
detection_pattern: Monitor for: (1) Unexpected response packets from internal IPs to programming function ports, (2) Malformed protocol messages with invalid data types or lengths in Shockwall logs, (3) Server process crashes or restart loops correlated with programming function calls, (4) Unusual memory consumption or CPU spikes during programming operations, (5) Authentication logs showing repeated failed validation errors, (6) Network packets with oversized or specially-crafted payloads to programming endpoints
mitigation_strategy: Implement strict input validation with whitelist-based acceptance criteria for all programming function inputs. Enforce data type checking, length limits, and format validation before processing. Deploy network segmentation to restrict programming function access to authorized management networks only. Enable request signing/HMAC verification. Implement rate limiting on programming endpoints. Deploy Web Application Firewall (WAF) rules to block malformed requests. Maintain input fuzzing tests in development pipeline. Apply principle of least privilege to service accounts. Monitor and alert on validation failures.

threat_vector: Qt SVG out-of-bounds write vulnerability in QPainterPath processing (CVE-2021-45930)
exploitation_method: Attacker crafts malicious SVG file with specially formed path data that triggers buffer overflow in QPathClipper or QPainterPath::addPath during SVG rendering. Out-of-bounds write in QtPrivate::QCommonArrayOps<QPainterPath::Element>::growAppend corrupts heap memory, potentially leading to code execution or crash.
detection_pattern: Monitor for: (1) Segmentation faults or heap corruption in Qt SVG rendering processes when processing specific SVG files, (2) ASAN/UBSAN alerts showing out-of-bounds write to QPainterPath::Element buffers, (3) Core dumps with stack traces containing qsvghandler.cpp or qpainterpath.cpp, (4) Abnormal memory access patterns during SVG parsing, (5) Application crashes when opening SVG files from untrusted sources
mitigation_strategy: Upgrade to Qt 5.15.3+ or Qt 6.2.2+ which contain out-of-bounds write fixes. Implement SVG input validation and sandboxing for SVG processing. Use memory-safe rendering contexts with address space layout randomization (ASLR). Disable SVG rendering if not required. Implement strict file format validation before passing to Qt SVG renderer. Monitor and restrict SVG file sources to trusted origins only.

threat_vector: ATT&CK T1559.002 - Inter-Process Communication: Dynamic Data Exchange (DDE)
exploitation_method: Attacker embeds DDE formulas in Microsoft Office documents (Word, Excel) that automatically execute when the document is opened. The formula triggers external command execution without macros, bypassing traditional macro security controls. Common payload: =cmd|'/c powershell.exe -Command [malicious_code]'!A1
detection_pattern: Monitor for: (1) Office process spawning cmd.exe or powershell.exe without user interaction, (2) DDE formula strings in document metadata or content: patterns containing '=cmd', '=powershell', '\x00DDE', (3) Unexpected network connections from winword.exe or excel.exe immediately after document open, (4) Windows Event ID 1 (Process Creation) showing Office parent process with suspicious child processes, (5) File modification timestamps mismatches between document creation and DDE object insertion
mitigation_strategy: Disable DDE: (1) Set Registry HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Office\[Version]\Word\Security DDE to 0 or use GPO to disable DDE expansion, (2) Configure Outlook/Word to open in Protected View by default, (3) Block DDE-capable file types at email gateway, (4) Use Application Control (AppLocker/WDAC) to prevent Office processes spawning shell processes, (5) Implement Antimalware Scan Interface (AMSI) monitoring for formula content, (6) Educate users to disable Field Code Updates in untrusted documents, (7) Update Office to latest patch level with DDE mitigations

threat_vector: Zoho ManageEngine Desktop Central <10.0.662 - Authenticated Information Disclosure via Reports Page
exploitation_method: Authenticated attacker navigates to Reports page and exploits improper access controls or SQL injection vulnerabilities to extract sensitive data from the backend database including credentials, configuration details, or system information
detection_pattern: Monitor for: unusual SQL queries in application logs from Reports functionality, database query errors with schema enumeration patterns, access logs showing authenticated users repeatedly accessing Reports with abnormal parameters, outbound data transfers from database queries, error messages revealing database structure, POST/GET requests to Reports endpoints with suspicious query parameters or multiple sequential requests with incrementing IDs
mitigation_strategy: Upgrade Zoho ManageEngine Desktop Central to version 10.0.662 or later; implement database access controls limiting Reports queries to necessary fields only; enable query audit logging; use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection; enforce least-privilege database accounts; restrict Reports page access to administrative roles; implement rate limiting on Reports endpoints; monitor and alert on unusual report generation patterns

threat_vector: Apache Pluto 3.1.0 MVCBean JSP portlet maven archetype - Unvalidated first name and last name fields vulnerable to reflected XSS
exploitation_method: Attacker injects malicious JavaScript code into the first name or last name input fields. When the portlet renders the user-supplied data without proper HTML encoding/escaping, the script executes in the victim's browser within the portlet context, potentially stealing session cookies, performing actions on behalf of the user, or redirecting to malicious sites.
detection_pattern: Monitor for: (1) HTML/JavaScript tags in first/last name fields (e.g.,
mitigation_strategy: Implement output encoding using OWASP ESAPI or Spring's HtmlUtils.htmlEscape() on all rendered user input. Apply HTML entity encoding to first/last name fields in JSP templates. Enforce Content Security Policy (CSP) headers to prevent inline script execution. Implement server-side input validation with allowlist approach for name fields (alphanumeric, spaces, hyphens only). Use auto-escaping templating engines. Apply HTTPOnly and Secure flags to session cookies. Upgrade to patched versions of Pluto framework when available.

threat_vector: Race condition in QoS (Quality of Service) request handler allowing concurrent add/update operations to trigger assertion due to improper validation in Snapdragon platform components
exploitation_method: Attacker sends multiple simultaneous QoS add or update requests to overwhelm validation logic, causing race conditions where concurrent operations bypass synchronization mechanisms, leading to invalid state and assertion failures that crash the service
detection_pattern: Monitor for: multiple QoS requests from same source within milliseconds; assertion/panic logs in QoS daemon; abnormal process termination of QoS service; kernel panic messages referencing QoS handler; sudden spike in concurrent connection attempts; log entries showing request validation failures followed by crashes; syslog entries with 'qos' component and SIGABRT signals
mitigation_strategy: Implement mutex/lock-based synchronization for QoS request handling; add input validation gates that serialize concurrent requests; implement request queuing mechanism with atomic state transitions; add bounds checking and pre-validation of request parameters; deploy watchdog process to restart crashed QoS service; apply latest firmware patches for Snapdragon components; implement rate limiting on QoS requests per source; add comprehensive logging at state transitions to detect race conditions during testing

threat_vector: ATT&CK T1027.011 - Embedded Payloads
exploitation_method: Attacker embeds malicious code or executable payloads within legitimate files (documents, images, archives, or application resources) to evade detection during transmission and storage. Payloads are extracted and executed at runtime through vulnerable parsers or intentional deobfuscation routines.
detection_pattern: Monitor for: unusual process creation from document/media applications; suspicious child process chains from Office/PDF readers; file entropy anomalies indicating compressed/encrypted sections; hex pattern signatures of known payload frameworks; unexpected network connections initiated by file processors; temporary file creation with executable permissions; memory injection attempts from benign processes; decompression/deobfuscation activities followed by execution.
mitigation_strategy: Implement application whitelisting for execution; disable dangerous file format features (macros, plugins, embedded objects) by default with organizational policy; use sandboxed document viewers; enforce code signing validation; deploy behavioral analysis to detect embedded payload extraction; maintain updated file format specifications and parsers; conduct regular file integrity monitoring; educate users on risks of untrusted file sources; utilize Content Disarm and Reconstruction (CDR) solutions to strip potentially malicious content; implement network-based payload detection using YARA rules and machine learning models trained on payload characteristics.

threat_vector: IBM Security Verify 10.0.0, 10.0.1.0, and 10.0.2.0 QR code generation input validation vulnerability (IBM X-Force ID: 212040)
exploitation_method: Attacker crafts malicious input containing special characters, path traversal sequences, or script payloads in QR code generation parameters. Due to insufficient input sanitization, the application processes and encodes sensitive data (tokens, URLs, credentials) into the QR code without proper validation, potentially exposing information in logs, cached files, or transmitted QR code data.
detection_pattern: Monitor for: (1) QR code generation requests with unusual character encodings or escape sequences in parameters; (2) Error messages containing sensitive data like tokens or API keys in application logs; (3) File system access to temporary directories containing QR code generation artifacts immediately after requests with suspicious payloads; (4) Unexpected HTTP response headers or body content containing plaintext credentials in QR-related endpoints; (5) Multiple failed QR generation attempts with progressively complex special character combinations
mitigation_strategy: Immediately upgrade to patched versions beyond 10.0.2.0 when available. Implement input validation whitelist for QR code generation parameters accepting only alphanumeric characters and necessary symbols. Sanitize all user-supplied input before QR encoding. Configure application logging to exclude sensitive data (tokens, credentials) from log output. Implement Content Security Policy headers. Monitor QR generation endpoints with WAF rules blocking requests containing SQL injection, path traversal, or encoding attack patterns. Apply principle of least privilege to service accounts generating QR codes. Conduct security code review of QR generation functionality.

threat_vector: Stored XSS in Bludit 3.13.1 TAGS section via login panel
exploitation_method: Attacker injects malicious JavaScript payload into the TAGS input field during login/authentication process. The payload is stored in the database and executed in the browser of any user who views the affected page, potentially stealing session cookies, credentials, or performing unauthorized actions on behalf of the victim.
detection_pattern: Monitor for: (1) HTML/JavaScript entities in TAGS database fields (script tags, event handlers like onerror, onload); (2) Web server logs showing POST requests to tag endpoints with suspicious payloads; (3) Browser console errors or unexpected script execution; (4) Database queries containing encoded JavaScript patterns; (5) Abnormal authentication logs with unusually long or encoded tag values
mitigation_strategy: Implement strict input validation and sanitization on all TAGS input fields: (1) Use whitelist approach allowing only alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores; (2) Apply HTML entity encoding on output before rendering; (3) Implement Content Security Policy (CSP) headers to prevent inline script execution; (4) Use context-aware output encoding; (5) Keep Bludit updated to patched versions; (6) Apply WAF rules to detect and block XSS payloads; (7) Perform regular security audits and code reviews of user input handling; (8) Educate users to avoid suspicious content in tags
